model_summary: Selects the six complaint-derived criteria (reduced positive reactivity, guilt about not enjoying, autopilot routines, broad emotional blunting, outward masking, and low motivation) as momentary EMA outcomes and pairs them with eight modifiable, measurable predictors drawn from high-ranked psycho-behavioural and bio-routine levers: behavioral activation, savoring/positive noticing, self-compassion, present-moment awareness, micro-activation energizers, sleep quality (morning diary), social connection, and phone screen-time (passive). Design emphasizes repeated momentary assessment (5 prompts/day) for within-person variance and a 28-day window (≈140 momentary observations + 28 morning diaries) to support gVAR estimation while minimizing redundant constructs.
variable_selection_notes: All six complaint-specific criteria are preserved to keep full clinical resolution. Predictor selection prioritized high HyDe / LLM signals (behavioral activation, savoring, self-compassion, mindfulness, sleep/circadian, micro-practices, social connection) and added an objective passive marker (phone screen time) to index autopilot/distracted behavior. Trade-offs: kept only one broad attention/presence variable (mindfulness/presence) to avoid collinearity with savoring/self-compassion and chose sleep as a daily diary rather than high-frequency passive sleep devices to reduce burden. Passive phone signal included despite potential nonlinearity because it is low-burden and mechanistically linked to autopilot/engagement. Uncertainty/bias: self-report measures may be biased by insight/effortful responding; passive screen time can reflect work demands rather than disengagement (hence flagged as nonlinear).

momentary_ema.notes: Fixed blocks with small random jitter reduce predictability while preserving coverage of daily routines. Use reminders and brief questions (≤ 60s).

daily_diary.notes: Morning diary captures prior-night sleep quality and overall overnight recovery; keep single-item 1–9 Likert for feasibility.

passive_streams.notes: Aggregate passive signals (phone screen minutes) over the interval 'since last prompt' to align with EMAs. Require opt-in and clear privacy notice.

design_recommendations.gvar_readiness_notes: With 5 prompts/day × 28 days (≈140 observations) per momentary variable and daily diaries, the dataset should be sufficient for initial gVAR (multivariate lagged models). Prefer within-person standardization (person-mean centering) before gVAR. Consider pruning predictors in follow-up analyses if collinearity inflates VAR parameter uncertainty.
design_recommendations.variance_support_notes: Use 1–9 anchored Likert scales for all EMA items to maximize within-person variance. Passive screen minutes are continuous and likely provide variance; morning sleep quality is expected to vary daily.
design_recommendations.missingness_risk_notes: Main risks: missed prompts (work/commute), passive stream opt-out, and morning diary noncompliance. Mitigation: brief EMAs, small incentives, adaptive reminder timing, and passive-only fallback if EMA missed (record passively).

criteria_variables.label: Diminished positive emotional response to pleasant events

measurement.item_or_signal: Since the last prompt, when something pleasant happened, how much did you feel pleasure or positive emotion? (1 = not at all, 9 = a lot)
measurement.notes: Anchored to positive-event reactivity; if no positive event occurred, instruct to rate 'if one had occurred, how much would you have felt' to avoid missingness.

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: person-mean centering; optionally seasonal detrend across weeks; no heavy nonlinear transform

criteria_variables.label: Guilt about not enjoying positive experiences

measurement.item_or_signal: Since the last prompt, to what extent did you feel guilty or self-critical about not enjoying something you experienced? (1 = not at all, 9 = extremely)
measurement.notes: Targets the specific guilt described in complaint; may correlate with general negative affect but captured as event-specific self-evaluation.

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: person-mean centering; consider logit-type transform if heavy floor effects

measurement.item_or_signal: Since the last prompt, how often did you do things in an automatic or 'on autopilot' way (mindless, not present)? (1 = not at all/present, 9 = entirely on autopilot)
measurement.notes: Directly operationalizes autopilot; pairs with passive screen-time to triangulate.

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: person-mean centering; consider robust scaling

measurement.item_or_signal: Since the last prompt, how intense were your emotions overall (both positive and negative)? (1 = not intense/very blunted, 9 = very intense)
measurement.notes: Captures broad affect intensity; useful to separate blunting from valence-specific changes.

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: person-mean centering; scale to unit variance

criteria_variables.label: External presentation of being 'fine' despite internal disengagement

measurement.item_or_signal: Since the last prompt, how much did you present or act as if you were 'fine' while feeling different inside? (1 = not at all, 9 = a lot)
measurement.notes: May show lower within-person variance but preserves complaint-specific outward masking behavior.

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: person-mean centering if variance sufficient; otherwise treat as slower-moving covariate

criteria_variables.label: Reduced motivation/interest to pursue non-routine pleasurable activities

measurement.item_or_signal: Since the last prompt, how motivated were you to do something enjoyable beyond routine obligations? (1 = not at all motivated, 9 = highly motivated)
measurement.notes: Designed to capture momentary fluctuations in motivational drive relevant to behavioral activation.

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: person-mean centering; consider AR components in gVAR

predictor_variables.label: Behavioral activation (intentional pleasurable activity engagement)
predictor_variables.mechanism_rationale: Scheduling and completing valued or pleasurable activities increases opportunities for positive reinforcement and can restore positive reactivity and motivation.
predictor_variables.intervention_examples_safe: Plan a short valued activity (call a friend, 15-min walk, creative hobby) and attempt it; start with graded/brief tasks.

measurement.item_or_signal: Since the last prompt, did you intentionally do a planned/enjoyable activity? Rate how engaged you were: 1 = not at all, 9 = fully engaged.
measurement.notes: If none planned/done, instruct to rate 1 for engagement; consider an optional event-flag field for binary occurrence.

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: person-mean centering; use in raw 1-9 for interpretability

expected_effects.rationale: Doing planned activities should increase momentary motivation/interest and future motivation via momentum.
Completing pleasurable activities provides opportunities for positive affective responses.

feasibility.notes: Single 1–9 item per prompt is low burden and aligns with EMA schedule.

predictor_variables.label: Savoring / positive-event noticing
predictor_variables.mechanism_rationale: Deliberate noticing and savoring of positive moments can amplify positive affective responses and counteract blunting.
predictor_variables.intervention_examples_safe: Brief savoring exercise (notice the sensory/details of a pleasant moment for 1-2 minutes).

measurement.item_or_signal: Since the last prompt, to what extent did you intentionally notice or savor any pleasant moment? (1 = not at all, 9 = very much)
measurement.notes: Captures brief micropractices linked to increased positive affect.

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: person-mean centering

expected_effects.rationale: Savoring should increase immediate positive affect when it occurs.

feasibility.notes: Short single-item EMA feasible.

predictor_variables.label: Self-compassion / reduced self-criticism
predictor_variables.mechanism_rationale: Self-compassion practices target guilt and self-criticism, reducing the secondary distress tied to not enjoying events.
predictor_variables.intervention_examples_safe: Brief self-compassion prompt (kind phrase to self; mindful acceptance).

measurement.item_or_signal: Since the last prompt, to what extent did you respond to yourself with compassion rather than self-criticism? (1 = entirely self-critical, 9 = entirely self-compassionate)
measurement.notes: Targets guilt-specific regulation skill; may moderate the impact of low positive reactivity on overall distress.

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: person-mean centering

expected_effects.rationale: Increased self-compassion should reduce guilt/self-criticism about low enjoyment.

feasibility.notes: Single-item assessments acceptable.

predictor_variables.label: Momentary present-moment awareness (mindfulness)
predictor_variables.mechanism_rationale: Increasing psychological presence reduces autopilot, increases emotional granularity, and supports noticing/savoring.
predictor_variables.intervention_examples_safe: Brief 30-second grounding check-in; orient to breath/body.

measurement.item_or_signal: Since the last prompt, how present and aware were you of what you were doing? (1 = not at all, 9 = completely present)
measurement.notes: Chosen as the single self-regulation/attention construct to limit redundancy.

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: person-mean centering

expected_effects.rationale: Greater presence should reduce autopilot-like behavior in subsequent moments.

feasibility.notes: Short single-item feasible and expected to vary within-day.

predictor_variables.label: Micro-activation (5-minute energizers / small wins)
predictor_variables.mechanism_rationale: Small, frequent activation tasks create momentum, improve energy and motivation, and provide opportunities for positive feedback.
predictor_variables.intervention_examples_safe: 5-minute brisk walk, stretch, music boost, or short social check-in.

measurement.item_or_signal: Since the last prompt, did you do a short energizing activity (5 minutes) and how energizing was it? (1 = not at all, 9 = very energizing)
measurement.notes: Captures micro-activations that can be rapidly deployed and measured repeatedly.

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: person-mean centering; consider binary event flag for sensitivity analyses

expected_effects.rationale: Micro-activations interrupt autopilot and increase subsequent engagement.

feasibility.notes: Frequent binary + intensity reporting possible; single 1–9 item reduces burden.

predictor_variables.label: Sleep quality / last-night restoration (morning diary)
predictor_variables.mechanism_rationale: Nightly sleep quality and circadian regularity influence daytime energy, attention, affect reactivity, and propensity to operate on autopilot.
predictor_variables.intervention_examples_safe: Consistent sleep window, light exposure in morning, pre-bed low-stimulation routine.

measurement.item_or_signal: How well did you sleep last night / how restorative was your sleep? (1 = very poor, 9 = very restorative)
measurement.notes: Single-item morning diary reduces burden and aligns with circadian hypothesis.

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: person-mean centering; consider controlling for weekday/weekend effects

expected_effects.rationale: Better sleep should reduce daytime automatic behavior and increase presence.

predictor_variables.risk_ethics_notes: If very poor sleep reported consistently, consider flagging for clinical follow-up.

feasibility.notes: Morning diary improves sleep measurement validity vs recall later in day.

predictor_variables.label: Social connection / brief social contact
predictor_variables.mechanism_rationale: Brief social contact and perceived connection can increase positive affect and motivation and act as behavioral activation.
predictor_variables.intervention_examples_safe: Short supportive message to a friend, 10-minute call, join a community event.

measurement.item_or_signal: Since the last prompt, how connected did you feel to others or did you have meaningful social contact? (1 = not at all, 9 = very connected)
measurement.notes: Captures brief social investments that can influence positive affect and motivation.

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: person-mean centering

expected_effects.rationale: Social connection increases opportunities for positive affect and reinforcement.

feasibility.notes: Single-item feasible; social contact may be constrained by schedule.

predictor_variables.label: Phone screen-time minutes (passive) — proxy for autopilot/distracted behavior
predictor_variables.mechanism_rationale: Higher passive phone screen time (unstructured) in short windows may indicate distractibility/autopilot and reduce presence or savoring; relationship may be nonlinear (work vs leisure).
predictor_variables.intervention_examples_safe: Phone-use boundary setting, brief phone-free periods, app timers.

measurement.channel: phone OS usage API (minutes screen ON/unlocked) aggregated in-app
measurement.item_or_signal: Total phone screen time minutes since previous prompt (auto-logged via OS usage API)
measurement.notes: Requires explicit consent to collect screen-time; interpret with caution (work-related usage vs autopilot leisure).

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: log1p(minutes) or winsorize extreme values; person-mean centering afterwards

expected_effects.rationale: Higher unstructured screen-time may be associated with more autopilot and less presence, but work-related screen time can confound directionality (hence nonlinear).

predictor_variables.risk_ethics_notes: Privacy-sensitive; require explicit opt-in and local aggregation (no raw content).

feasibility.notes: Feasible on most smartphones but may be limited by OS permissions and user comfort.

edges.notes: Behavioral activation increases subsequent motivation/interest.
Savoring produces near-term boosts in positive reactivity.
Self-compassion reduces guilt/self-criticism.
Momentary presence interrupts autopilot in short windows.
Better sleep reduces daytime automatic behavior and improves presence.

expected_collinearity_risks: P02 (savoring) and P01 (behavioral activation) may correlate when activation includes pleasant activities.
P04 (mindfulness/presence) and P03 (self-compassion) may show moderate correlation in practice (both attention-related regulation skills).
Several EMA outcomes (C01, C04) may covary strongly; consider dimensionality reduction if necessary.
expected_floor_ceiling_risks: C05 (external presentation) may show floor/ceiling effects in some participants (low variability).
P03 (self-compassion) and P02 (savoring) may show skew (many low baseline values) early in the study.
variance_enhancement_suggestions: Use 1–9 Likert anchors and explicit instruction to respond in hypothetical terms if no event occurred to reduce zero-inflation.
Encourage short planned micro-experiments to produce variance (e.g., daily planned pleasant activity).
excluded_or_deprioritized_candidates: Multiple near-duplicate self-regulation constructs (e.g., both lengthy mindfulness scales and brief presence item) — only brief presence included.
Objective continuous sleep device streams (e.g., actigraphy) — excluded to reduce burden and complexity; single-item morning diary included instead.
diagnostics.gvar_feasibility_summary: With 5 prompts/day across momentary variables and 28 days, the within-person time series should provide adequate temporal resolution for gVAR. Person-mean centering and controlling for day-of-week/sleep trends recommended. If multicollinearity arises, drop or combine highly correlated predictors (e.g., P01 vs P05) before final gVAR estimation.

safety_notes: No acute self-harm or psychosis signals present in the complaint. If responses during data collection indicate severe distress, suicidal ideation, or acute sleep deprivation with cognitive impairment, trigger safety protocol directing participant to urgent clinical contact. Passive data collection (screen time) requires clear consent and privacy safeguards.

model_summary: Goal: model the complaint 'avoids social stuff because convinced I'm annoying → post-event rumination → cancel plans → loneliness → self-criticism' with a compact, estimable gVAR-ready set. Criteria (C01–C06) are the six atomic, targetable outcomes from the complaint measured as end-of-day diary items. Predictors (P01–P08) are modifiable psychosocial levers prioritized from HyDe/LLM signals: behavioral activation/exposure, cognitive restructuring, rumination-interruption (mindfulness/decentering), micro-connection planning, self-compassion practice, sleep quality, social-support mobilization, and a passive social-activity signal. Sampling design uses 5 momentary EMA prompts/day for within-day predictors, a morning sleep diary, and an end-of-day diary for criteria; passive social-behavior aggregated daily. The set balances coverage (beliefs, behavior, cognitive process, social support, recovery factors) with low redundancy to remain estimable by gVAR over ~28 days (~140 momentary observations + daily diaries).
variable_selection_notes: Selected predictors emphasize high-ranked PSYCHO/SOCIAL approaches from HyDe and LLM mappings (behavioral activation, CBT/exposure, mindfulness for rumination, social-prescribing-style micro-connections, and self-compassion). Sleep and passive social-activity were included because the post-global and per-criterion signals frequently prioritized sleep/circadian supports and objective behavior measures. Trade-offs: avoided adding multiple near-duplicate self-regulation variables (kept one broad rumination-interruption variable and one self-compassion variable), and combined behavioral-experiments/exposure under a single behavior-activation predictor to reduce collinearity. Uncertainty/bias notes: relevance scores favor psychosocial interventions (given ranking signals) and downweight biomedical/neuromodulation candidates (intentionally deprioritized here for feasibility and safety). Some predictor→criterion effects (e.g., passive social-activity) may be nonlinear (presence of contact could reflect high anxiety or healthy engagement), so modeled as nonlinear/unknown where appropriate.

momentary_ema.notes: 5 prompts/day provides a balance of within-day resolution for rumination, social attempts, and support-seeking while keeping burden moderate. Use small random jitter within each block to reduce predictability.

daily_diary.notes: End-of-day diary collects the six criteria to align with the user's summary of the day's social behavior and feelings.

passive_streams.notes: Passive social-activity (counts/minutes) aggregated per 24h window to protect privacy and reduce data sparsity; linked to same-day end-of-day diary.

design_recommendations.gvar_readiness_notes: Expected ~140 momentary + 28 daily + 28 passive daily aggregates = ~196 usable rows per variable (momentary variables: 140 obs each; daily variables: 28 obs). gVAR will need at least ~100-150 repeated observations for stable within-person estimates; 28 days with 5 prompts/day meets this for momentary predictors. Recommend participant-level z-scoring prior to gVAR and inclusion of time (day) fixed effects if trends observed.
design_recommendations.variance_support_notes: Use 1–9 anchored Likert scales for EMA items to increase within-person variance. For rare events (cancellations), capture intensity/impact via Likert plus an optional numeric count in daily diary.
design_recommendations.missingness_risk_notes: Main risk: participant burden leading to missed prompts (especially later in day). Mitigate with short items (1–2 questions per prompt), reminders, and small incentives. Passive data may have gaps due to permissions; ensure consent and initial testing.

measurement.item_or_signal: Today, to what extent did you avoid or withdraw from social activities (declined, did not attend, or withdrew) because of worries about how others saw you?
measurement.response_scale_or_unit: 1-9 Likert (1 = Not at all, 9 = Very much)
measurement.notes: Also optionally capture a small integer 'number of plans cancelled' in the same daily diary for event-count context (0,1,2+).

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: z-score per-person; counts log1p if used

measurement.item_or_signal: Today, how strongly did you hold the belief 'I am annoying' or expect others to react unfavorably?
measurement.response_scale_or_unit: 1-9 Likert (1 = Not at all, 9 = Extremely strongly)
measurement.notes: Belief intensity can trend; include time covariates and consider detrending prior to gVAR.

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: person-mean centering then z-score

measurement.item_or_signal: How much did you replay or get stuck on things you said in social situations (e.g., 'why did I say that?') today?
measurement.response_scale_or_unit: 1-9 Likert (1 = Not at all, 9 = A great deal)
measurement.notes: Momentary rumination captured by momentary EMA predictors; this criterion is aggregated daily to reflect the user's described 48-hour linger.

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: person-mean centering; consider AR(1) residual structure in gVAR

measurement.item_or_signal: Today, how much did you cancel or withdraw from planned social engagements (and how impactful was it for you)?
measurement.response_scale_or_unit: 1-9 Likert (1 = None/not impactful, 9 = Many cancellations/high impact)
measurement.notes: Also include optional numeric count of canceled plans (0,1,2+).

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: z-score; log1p for counts

criteria_variables.label: Experienced loneliness from reduced social contact

measurement.item_or_signal: How lonely did you feel today as a result of reduced social contact?
measurement.response_scale_or_unit: 1-9 Likert (1 = Not at all lonely, 9 = Extremely lonely)
measurement.notes: Loneliness often fluctuates daily and responds to brief social contacts; good candidate for gVAR coupling with same-day predictors.

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: person-mean centering then z-score

measurement.item_or_signal: When you felt lonely today, how much did you respond with self-criticism or calling yourself 'dumb' for being lonely?
measurement.response_scale_or_unit: 1-9 Likert (1 = Not at all, 9 = Extremely self-critical)
measurement.notes: Captures the described downstream self-blame after loneliness.

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: z-score; consider interaction terms with loneliness

predictor_variables.label: Behavioral activation / social exposure (behavioral experiments)
predictor_variables.mechanism_rationale: Directly counteracts avoidance by increasing opportunities for disconfirmatory social experiences; reduces cancellations and, over time, loneliness and negative beliefs.
predictor_variables.intervention_examples_safe: Planned small social tasks (attend one low-pressure social event; say hello to a colleague); graded exposure steps.

measurement.item_or_signal: Since the last prompt, to what extent did you purposefully attempt to engage with others or accept/attend social opportunities (graded steps/exposures)?
measurement.response_scale_or_unit: 1-9 Likert (1 = Not at all, 9 = A great deal)
measurement.notes: Short single-item EMA per prompt; link with event flags for actual attendance counts in daily diary.

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: person-mean centering; z-score

expected_effects.rationale: Immediate reductions in avoidance likelihood and same-day attendance; rapid effect within hours.
Should reduce cancellations and their impact within same day or next day.
Increases contact and reduces loneliness across same to next day.

feasibility.notes: High feasibility; standard single-item EMA.

predictor_variables.label: Cognitive restructuring / thought-challenging for 'I'm annoying'
predictor_variables.mechanism_rationale: Directly targets the maladaptive belief that drives avoidance and post-event rumination by generating alternative appraisals and testing predictions.
predictor_variables.intervention_examples_safe: Cognitive reframing, evidence-for/evidence-against worksheets, brief self-questioning prompts.

measurement.item_or_signal: Since the last prompt, to what extent did you actively challenge or reframe thoughts like 'I'm annoying'?
measurement.response_scale_or_unit: 1-9 Likert (1 = Not at all, 9 = A great deal)
measurement.notes: Captures usage of cognitive skills; may trend as skills are learned.

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: person-mean centering; z-score

expected_effects.rationale: Belief change may accumulate over hours to days as cognitive work generalizes.
Active reframing can reduce immediate rumination episodes.

feasibility.notes: Skill-level variable; moderate within-person variance expected.

predictor_variables.label: Rumination-interruption / mindfulness-decentering (post-event decompression)
predictor_variables.mechanism_rationale: Teaches rapid decentering and set-shifting to interrupt perseverative replay of social interactions, reducing duration/intensity of rumination.
predictor_variables.intervention_examples_safe: Brief grounding, 3-minute breathing or labeling thoughts, 'notice-thought-let-go' exercises after social events.

measurement.item_or_signal: Since the last prompt, how often were you able to step out of replaying social interactions (interrupt or let go of ruminative thoughts)?
measurement.response_scale_or_unit: 1-9 Likert (1 = Not at all, 9 = Very often/successfully)
measurement.notes: Captures momentary skill use; expected to have strong contemporaneous relation with reported rumination intensity.

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: z-score

expected_effects.rationale: Immediate interruption reduces rumination intensity rapidly.
Less rumination reduces downstream self-criticism within same day.

feasibility.notes: Brief EMAs feasible and low burden.

predictor_variables.label: Micro-connection planning (stepwise reengagement / social-prescribing micro-contacts)
predictor_variables.mechanism_rationale: Structured small social contacts (texts, short calls) lower friction for reengagement and can rapidly reduce loneliness and prevent cancellations by building momentum.
predictor_variables.intervention_examples_safe: Planned one-text check-ins, five-minute coffee catch-ups, scheduled short activities with a known person.

measurement.item_or_signal: Since the last prompt, to what extent did you attempt small, intentional social connections (texts, short calls, brief in-person micro-interactions)?
measurement.response_scale_or_unit: 1-9 Likert (1 = Not at all, 9 = Many/very engaged)
measurement.notes: Designed to capture low-cost social moves that reduce loneliness quickly.

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: z-score; counts log1p if numeric counts are recorded

expected_effects.rationale: Micro-connections produce rapid, often same-day reductions in loneliness.
Repeated small contacts reduce avoidance by building social momentum.

feasibility.notes: EMA captures micro-actions reliably.

predictor_variables.label: Self-compassion practice (response to self-criticism)
predictor_variables.mechanism_rationale: Teaches alternative self-relating to reduce self-criticism after loneliness/cancellations; may moderate the impact of loneliness on mood and help-seeking.
predictor_variables.intervention_examples_safe: Short compassionate self-statements, guided compassionate imagery, same-day compassionate prompts.

measurement.item_or_signal: Since the last prompt, to what extent did you respond to self-critical thoughts with compassionate statements or soothing actions?
measurement.response_scale_or_unit: 1-9 Likert (1 = Not at all, 9 = Very much)
measurement.notes: Likely to moderate relationship between loneliness and self-criticism.

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: z-score

expected_effects.rationale: Compassionate responding reduces intensity of self-criticism within the same day.

feasibility.notes: EMA feasible; training expected to change baseline over weeks.

predictor_variables.label: Sleep quality / circadian alignment (last night)
predictor_variables.mechanism_rationale: Sleep and circadian health modulate affective reactivity and perseverative cognition; improved sleep can reduce rumination and increase capacity for social engagement.
predictor_variables.intervention_examples_safe: Consistent sleep timing, wind-down routines, light exposure adjustments.

measurement.item_or_signal: How well did you sleep last night overall?
measurement.notes: Optionally complement with passive sleep duration from wearable if available.

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: z-score; winsorize extreme values

expected_effects.rationale: Poor sleep increases rumination; better sleep reduces ruminative reactivity within 1–2 days.

feasibility.notes: Morning diary is low burden; wearables optional.

predictor_variables.label: Social support mobilization (asking for help / confiding)
predictor_variables.mechanism_rationale: Actively reaching out or mobilizing support reduces loneliness and reinforces corrective feedback about social perceptions.
predictor_variables.intervention_examples_safe: Reaching out to a friend, sending a message describing feelings, scheduling a supportive call.

measurement.item_or_signal: Since the last prompt, to what extent did you seek support or confide in someone about how you were feeling?
measurement.response_scale_or_unit: 1-9 Likert (1 = Not at all, 9 = A great deal)
measurement.notes: Captures active help-seeking which often reduces loneliness and negative beliefs.

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: z-score

expected_effects.rationale: Support-seeking tends to reduce loneliness quickly when successful.

feasibility.notes: Feasible by EMA; may vary by social context.

predictor_variables.label: Passive social-activity (outgoing contacts / social app minutes) — daily aggregate
predictor_variables.mechanism_rationale: Objective measure of social behavior: calls, messages, or time in social apps. Acts as a behavioral proxy for engagement and complements self-report (may be nonlinear: high use can signal anxiety or healthy engagement).
predictor_variables.intervention_examples_safe: N/A — passive measurement only; participant consent required.

measurement.channel: smartphone (permissioned activity logs or aggregate counts) / behavioral_log
measurement.item_or_signal: Daily count of outgoing social interactions (calls + distinct addressed messages) and minutes spent in social communication apps (24h aggregate).
measurement.notes: Privacy-preserving aggregates only; interpret carefully because high counts may reflect coping via online rumination rather than healthy connection.

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: log1p for counts/minutes; person-mean centering

expected_effects.rationale: Increased social activity often reduces loneliness, but may be nonlinearly related (passive scrolling vs active calls differ).

predictor_variables.risk_ethics_notes: Ensure consent for passive logging and clear privacy controls.

feasibility.notes: Feasibility depends on permissions and platform variability; missing data risk if permissions revoked.

edges.notes: Behavioral activation expected to reduce avoidance within hours to a day.
Cognitive restructuring reduces belief strength over days as cognitive reframes generalize.
Mindfulness-based interruption yields rapid decreases in rumination intensity.
Micro-connections expected to reduce loneliness same day.
Self-compassion practice directly reduces self-criticism.
Support-seeking reduces loneliness if contact is effective.

expected_collinearity_risks: P01 (behavioral activation) vs P04 (micro-connections) and P07 (support mobilization) — all capture social approach behavior but differ in scale/intent; expect moderate collinearity.
P02 (cognitive restructuring) vs P05 (self-compassion) — both target internal response to events; moderate collinearity possible but mechanisms differ (reappraisal vs soothing).
expected_floor_ceiling_risks: C02 (belief 'I am annoying') may show slow gradual change and potential floor/ceiling at extremes; consider detrending.
P08 (passive social activity) counts may be zero-inflated for some participants; use log1p and consider zero-inflated diagnostics.
variance_enhancement_suggestions: If within-person variance is low for belief (C02), increase number of daily cognitive-challenge prompts or add brief subjective-event flags to capture belief-activation moments.
Record optional event flags (e.g., 'had a social interaction') so that predictors can be linked to events and increase effective variability.
excluded_or_deprioritized_candidates: Biomedical/neuromodulation interventions (TMS, pharmacotherapy) were deprioritized for this initial observational gVAR due to feasibility, safety, and the psychosocial focus of the complaint.
Multiple near-duplicate self-regulation constructs were avoided (kept one rumination-interruption and one self-compassion variable).
diagnostics.gvar_feasibility_summary: With 28 days and 5 momentary prompts/day, momentary predictors will have ~140 within-person observations — suitable for individual-level gVAR. Daily criteria (28 obs) are fewer but acceptable when combined with dense momentary predictors; consider vector-autoregression with mixed sampling (use VAR-extended or downsample momentary predictors to daily aggregates for criterion-focused models). Recommend person-mean centering and AR(1) residual modeling; check stationarity and include time trend if needed.

safety_notes: No acute self-harm or severe risk was described in the complaint. Standard safety precautions: include an initial risk screen (suicidal ideation, severe deterioration) before enrollment, provide clear instructions for seeking professional help if distress escalates, and ensure passive data collection consent and privacy protections. If participants report incapacitating anxiety or severe functional decline, escalate to clinical evaluation.

model_summary: Select all six complaint-derived criteria (health-focused worry, catastrophic symptom interpretation, compulsive internet checking, failure to inhibit checking, perceived loss of control, acute panic on symptom onset) and seven modifiable, high-signal predictors drawn from the PSYCHO domain: cognitive restructuring/probabilistic thinking (P01), scheduled checking & digital-hygiene (P02), interoceptive exposure (P03), mindfulness/acceptance & distress-tolerance (P04), executive-inhibition/urge-control training (P05), sleep/circadian stabilization (P06), and social support/accountability (P07). Measurement uses intensive EMA (6 prompts/day, 28 days) for momentary state and skill-practice adherence, a single morning diary for sleep anchoring, and optional passive phone streams (per-prompt aggregation of screen minutes and unlock counts). The design prioritizes within-person variance, minimal collinearity (one broad self-regulation/inhibition predictor), clear recall windows, and a feasible observation count for later gVAR estimation (≈168 momentary observations + 28 morning diaries).
variable_selection_notes: All six complaint-derived criteria (C01–C06) are retained to preserve the complaint's full resolution and to enable multivariate outcome modeling. Predictors were chosen to (1) map directly to high-ranked HyDe / LLM solution nodes (cognitive restructuring, metacognitive/attention training, exposure/urge-work, digital hygiene, inhibitory control, sleep, social accountability), (2) be modifiable by brief interventions or behavioral routines, and (3) have realistic EMA or passive measures with expected within-person variability. Trade-offs: many HyDe nodes are closely related (e.g., various CBT skill families); to reduce collinearity we merged overlapping concepts into single, broader predictors (e.g., P01 covers cognitive restructuring and probabilistic thinking; P04 covers mindfulness/acceptance/distress tolerance). Passive measures (screen minutes/unlocks) are included but optional (consent-dependent) because they increase objectivity for checking behavior but risk missingness. Uncertainty/bias notes: self-report of checking duration may be biased; passive phone signals are a proxy (not content-specific) and may miss non-phone checking. We prioritized constructs most directly tied to the complaint's mechanisms (catastrophizing, threat monitoring, reassurance-seeking/compulsivity, urge-inhibition, arousal) to keep the model estimable for gVAR.

momentary_ema.notes: Six prompts/day balances temporal resolution (0-6h lag sensitivity) and participant burden. Blocks include jitter to avoid predictability. EMA items below are asked at each prompt unless otherwise noted.

daily_diary.notes: Morning diary captures prior-night sleep timing and quality for P06 (sleep/circadian stabilization).

passive_streams.notes: If participant consents, collect phone OS aggregated screen_time_minutes and screen_unlock_count; aggregate into the same recall window as EMA (since last prompt). Passive data increases objectivity for checking behavior but may be unavailable for some participants (iOS/Android privacy limits).

design_recommendations.gvar_readiness_notes: Expected ~168 momentary observations + 28 daily diaries per participant (if adherence high). This should be sufficient for gVAR with 13 time-series (6 criteria + 7 predictors) after variable selection/reduction; initial gVAR may require pruning to the strongest predictors or dimension reduction if effective sample drops below targets.
design_recommendations.variance_support_notes: Use 1–9 anchored Likert for subjective items to maximize within-person variance. For duration-oriented items (checking) record self-reported minutes (continuous) and an intensity/urge 1–9 scale to avoid rare-event issues.
design_recommendations.missingness_risk_notes: Main risks: passive stream opt-out, skipped EMA prompts, and missing passive windows (OS restrictions). Recommend push reminders, brief items (<30s), and incentives for adherence. Plan for multiple imputation and time-series appropriate missing-data methods; include per-prompt passive-availability flags.

measurement.item_or_signal: Since the last prompt, how much have you worried about your health? (1 = Not at all, 9 = Extremely)
measurement.notes: Captures momentary fluctuation in worry; asked each prompt.

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: person-mean centering and z-scoring per participant; consider detrending if systematic drift

criteria_variables.label: Catastrophic interpretation of minor bodily symptoms

measurement.item_or_signal: Since the last prompt, if you noticed bodily sensations, to what extent did you interpret them as indicating something very serious (e.g., convinced you might be dying)? (1 = Not at all, 9 = Completely)
measurement.response_scale_or_unit: 1-9 Likert (anchored) plus conditional event flag if no sensation
measurement.notes: If no sensations occurred, record 1 on intensity and set event_flag=false. This measures appraisal intensity rather than trait belief.

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: person-mean centering; treat as approx-continuous

measurement.channel: smartphone EMA app + optional passive phone sensor
measurement.item_or_signal: Since the last prompt, did you search for health information online? If yes, how many minutes did you spend? (enter minutes). Also rate how strong the urge was on a 1-9 scale (1 = No urge, 9 = Very strong urge).
measurement.notes: If passive consent is given, also collect screen_time_minutes_browser_since_last_prompt and screen_unlock_count as passive proxies to cross-validate self-report.

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: log1p(minutes) for skewed duration; person-mean center urge ratings

criteria_variables.label: Failure to inhibit checking despite recognizing irrationality

measurement.item_or_signal: Since the last prompt, to what extent did you try and fail to resist checking/searching even though you knew it was irrational? (1 = Not at all, 9 = Completely)
measurement.notes: Captures repeated engagement and perceived failure to inhibit; complements objective duration measures.

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: person-mean centering; consider binary flag for any failure events if distribution highly zero-inflated

criteria_variables.label: Subjective sense of loss of control and distress about it

measurement.item_or_signal: Since the last prompt, how much did you feel that your health worries were out of control and how distressed did that make you? (1 = Not at all, 9 = Extremely)
measurement.notes: Captures perceived control and associated distress; central to user's complaint.

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: person-mean centering and z-score

criteria_variables.label: Acute panic-level fear when a symptom appears

measurement.item_or_signal: If you noticed a bodily symptom since the last prompt, how intense was your immediate fear/panic? (1 = Not at all, 9 = Extreme panic). Also mark event_flag if a symptom occurred.
measurement.notes: Event-anchored intensity is recorded; if no symptom, intensity = 1 and event_flag=false. This supports modeling of cue-triggered panic events.

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: person-mean centering; consider event-based models for event_flag-conditioned analyses

predictor_variables.label: Cognitive restructuring / probabilistic thinking practice
predictor_variables.mechanism_rationale: Teaches reappraisal and probabilistic estimates to reduce catastrophic interpretations and global health worry; directly targets appraisal processes.
predictor_variables.intervention_examples_safe: Brief guided reappraisal exercises (identify thought, evidence for/against, alternative explanations; probability estimation exercises).

measurement.item_or_signal: Since the last prompt, did you use a cognitive reappraisal/probability-checking strategy? If yes, rate how effective it was (1=Not at all,9=Very effective) and record minutes spent practicing.
measurement.response_scale_or_unit: binary + 1-9 Likert for effectiveness + minutes practiced
measurement.notes: Practice frequency and perceived effectiveness will be used as time-varying predictors; minutes practice often low but effective for short-term shifts.

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: use minutes (log1p) and effectiveness (person-mean center); binary practice flag as additional covariate

expected_effects.rationale: Reappraisal reduces momentary worry; effects can accumulate over hours.
Directly targets catastrophic interpretation schemas.
Reducing threat appraisals should reduce checking driven by reassurance-seeking, though effect may be indirect and slower.
Increased cognitive control over appraisals supports resisting checks.
Improved appraisals increase perceived influence and reduce distress about loss of control.
Lower catastrophic appraisals can reduce panic intensity when symptoms occur.

predictor_variables.risk_ethics_notes: Low risk; ensure exercises do not increase avoidance. If exposure is used in conjunction, supervise.

feasibility.notes: Self-report practice and minutes are easy to collect; expected to vary within-person across days.

predictor_variables.label: Scheduled checking & digital hygiene (if-then plans and limits)
predictor_variables.mechanism_rationale: Creates rules and boundaries for information-search behavior, reducing compulsive, unstructured checking and long search sessions.
predictor_variables.intervention_examples_safe: Set specific 20-minute scheduled windows for health information seeking, use website blockers, implement 'urge-if/then' plans.

measurement.channel: smartphone EMA app + optional passive phone sensor
measurement.item_or_signal: Since the last prompt, did you check health information outside scheduled windows? (Y/N). Self-reported minutes spent searching and perceived adherence to schedule on 1-9 scale. Passive proxy: browser_time_minutes_since_last_prompt and screen_unlock_count.
measurement.response_scale_or_unit: binary + minutes + 1-9 adherence Likert; passive minutes if available
measurement.notes: Pairs with passive streams to capture objective phone activity; ideal for modeling immediate antecedents of checking episodes.

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: log1p(minutes) for durations; use adherence Likert person-mean centered

expected_effects.rationale: Reduced uncontrolled searching reduces maintenance of worry over the day.
Sometimes brief checking reduces uncertainty; excessive unstructured checking increases catastrophic interpretations — effect may be nonlinear.
Directly targets checking duration; close temporal effects expected.
Scheduled checking and if-then plans increase capacity to inhibit checking impulses.
Regaining behavioral control should increase perceived control over time.
Indirect effect via reduced reassurance-seeking; timing uncertain.

predictor_variables.risk_ethics_notes: If implementing website blockers, ensure user can access health services in case of real concerns.

feasibility.notes: Passive phone metrics improve measurement but require consent and may be unavailable on some devices.

predictor_variables.label: Interoceptive exposure to feared bodily sensations
predictor_variables.mechanism_rationale: Repeated planned exposure to benign bodily sensations reduces catastrophic conditioned fear and panic reactivity via inhibitory learning.
predictor_variables.intervention_examples_safe: Guided low-intensity interoceptive exercises (light hyperventilation-like exercises, spinning head movements in low-intensity controlled setting) with grounding and safety plan.

measurement.item_or_signal: Since the last prompt, did you practice interoceptive exposure exercises? (Y/N). If yes, minutes practiced and peak fear during exercise (1-9).
measurement.notes: Exposure sessions may be infrequent initially; capture both practice and intensity to model habituation effects.

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: minutes: log1p; peak fear: person-mean centering

expected_effects.rationale: Repeated exposure reduces baseline worry over days via extinction/inhibitory learning.
Targets catastrophic associations directly; reductions expected after sessions.
Less fear-driven checking expected as interoceptive tolerance improves.
Greater tolerance supports resisting checking impulses.
Mastery of bodily tolerance increases perceived control over time.
Primarily reduces acute panic reactivity to bodily cues.

predictor_variables.risk_ethics_notes: Exposure can transiently increase arousal; provide safety instructions and stop rules. Avoid high-intensity exercises without clinician support.

feasibility.notes: Practice sessions may be sparse; consider modeling session-level effects with multi-timescale lags.

predictor_variables.label: Mindfulness/acceptance & distress-tolerance skills
predictor_variables.mechanism_rationale: Reduces reactivity and fused identification with worry and bodily sensations; increases urge-surfing capacity.
predictor_variables.intervention_examples_safe: Brief mindful body scans, urge-surfing instructions, acceptance prompts, and paced breathing practices.

measurement.item_or_signal: Since the last prompt, did you use a mindfulness/acceptance or breathing exercise? If yes, how many minutes and rate immediate relief (1-9).
measurement.notes: Mindfulness practice frequency and perceived relief will be used to model short-term downregulation of arousal and urges.

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: minutes log1p; relief rating person-mean center

expected_effects.rationale: Reduces momentary worry via downregulating arousal and shifting attention.
Decentering reduces fusion with catastrophic thoughts.
May reduce urge-driven checking in the short term by increasing tolerance.
Improves ability to tolerate urges and resist checking.
Less reactivity reduces distress about loss of control.
Breathing and mindfulness can acutely lower panic intensity.

predictor_variables.risk_ethics_notes: Low risk; ensure guidance for participants with trauma history (use grounding techniques).

feasibility.notes: High feasibility; short practices easily tracked by EMA.

predictor_variables.label: Executive function / inhibition training (urge pause & response prevention)
predictor_variables.mechanism_rationale: Specific practice of inhibitory control and urge delay reduces compulsive checking and failure-to-inhibit behaviors.
predictor_variables.intervention_examples_safe: Short stop-and-think routines, if-then delay plans, brief computerized inhibitory-control exercises (where available).

measurement.item_or_signal: Since the last prompt, did you use an urge-pause/inhibition exercise when you felt the urge to check? (Y/N). If yes, rate success resisting (1-9) and minutes practiced.
measurement.notes: Directly targets C03 and C04; measure perceived success and practice minutes.

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: success rating person-mean center; minutes log1p

expected_effects.rationale: By reducing checking, worry maintenance may reduce over hours.
Less compulsive checking weakens reinforcement of catastrophic beliefs.
Direct immediate effect on checking episodes.
Directly trains inhibition; expected strong short-term effects.

Successful inhibition increases perceived control (polarity: higher inhibition practice increases control -> lower C05; sign reported relative to predictor: more P05 -> lower C05 so expected_sign is '-' but we include '+' only if variable denotes failure; to avoid confusion, we use '-' below).
Reducing habitual checking and improving inhibition may reduce panic escalation in some contexts.

predictor_variables.risk_ethics_notes: Low risk. For computerized inhibitory training, ensure it's brief and optional.

feasibility.notes: Feasible for EMA; effects may manifest at multiple timescales.

predictor_variables.label: Sleep and circadian stabilization
predictor_variables.mechanism_rationale: Better sleep reduces baseline anxiety and arousal reactivity, indirectly reducing worry and panic vulnerability.
predictor_variables.intervention_examples_safe: Consistent sleep/wake schedule, reduce nocturnal checking, pre-sleep constructive worry planning.

measurement.item_or_signal: Morning diary: last night's sleep onset time, wake time, sleep quality (1-9). Also nightly adherence to sleep routine (1-9). Optional passive: sleep_duration_minutes via wearable / phone (if available).
measurement.notes: Daily morning diary anchors sleep measures; sleep changes will be modeled as a slow-moving predictor (24-72h effects).

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: sleep minutes: z-score per participant; quality: person-mean center

expected_effects.rationale: Improved sleep reduces baseline anxiety across days.
Lower arousal reduces catastrophic appraisals.
Less fatigue may reduce impulsive checking, though indirect.
Better sleep may improve inhibition capacity (operationalized as decreased C04). For clarity, expected_sign here is '-' (more P06 -> lower C04).
Improved sleep supports sense of control and reduces distress.
Sleep effects on panic vulnerability often manifest across nights.

predictor_variables.risk_ethics_notes: Recommend medical evaluation if serious sleep disorder suspected.

feasibility.notes: Daily diary makes this feasible; passive wearable increases accuracy but is optional.

predictor_variables.label: Social support & accountability mechanisms
predictor_variables.mechanism_rationale: Brief social accountability (check-ins, buddy plans) can reduce avoidance-driven worry and provide alternative feedback to catastrophizing.
predictor_variables.intervention_examples_safe: Daily check-in with a trusted person or accountability partner about scheduled checking & coping plans.

measurement.item_or_signal: Since the last prompt, did you reach out to or receive a supportive/check-in message from your accountability partner? (Y/N). Rate perceived helpfulness 1-9 and whether they reminded you of your plan (Y/N).
measurement.notes: Social reminders often moderate urge-driven behaviors; variability occurs across days.

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: binary flags as-is; helpfulness person-mean center

expected_effects.rationale: Support reduces immediate worry through reassurance and plan reminders (when adaptive).
External reality checks can help challenge catastrophic appraisals in the moment.
Accountability reduces unplanned checking episodes.
Support can help enact inhibition plans when urges arise.
Regular social support improves perceived control and reduces distress about losing control.
May help reduce panic intensity situationally but effects are context-dependent.

predictor_variables.risk_ethics_notes: Ensure confidentiality planning; avoid recommending reliance on untrained others for medical advice.

feasibility.notes: Feasible as EMA flags; actual social-contact variability depends on participant network.

edges.notes: Cognitive restructuring expected to reduce catastrophic interpretations within hours to a day.
Scheduled checking and digital hygiene should quickly reduce unplanned checking episodes and duration.
Interoceptive exposure directly targets panic reactivity to bodily cues; effects can be immediate to several days post-session.
Mindfulness/acceptance increases tolerance and decreases failure-to-inhibit checking.
Inhibition/urge-control training directly targets failure to resist checking; strong immediate effects possible.
Sleep stabilization is a slower-moving predictor reducing baseline worry across days.
Social support and accountability can rapidly improve perceived control and reduce distress about loss of control.

expected_collinearity_risks: P01 (cognitive restructuring) and P04 (mindfulness) can correlate—both target appraisal/attention processes; monitor VIF and consider residualizing or combining if very high.
P02 (scheduled checking) and passive screen_time proxies for C03 will be correlated; treat passive minutes and self-report minutes as complementary but watch collinearity.
P05 (inhibition training) overlaps mechanistically with P04 (distress tolerance) but differs in action orientation; moderate risk.
expected_floor_ceiling_risks: C06 (panic intensity) may be zero-inflated if symptoms are infrequent; capture event_flag and consider event-conditioned modeling.
P03 interoceptive exposure minutes may be sparse early, creating floor effects—model as session-level binary + continuous minutes when present.
variance_enhancement_suggestions: Use 1–9 Likert anchored scales to increase variance for subjective constructs.
Collect both duration (minutes) and intensity (1–9) for checking episodes to avoid rare-event binary problems.
Encourage at least brief daily practice of P01/P04 to increase within-person variance in predictors.
excluded_or_deprioritized_candidates: Many BIO neuromodulation/pharmacology nodes (TMS, meds) are deprioritized at this behavioral early-intervention stage for feasibility and safety in EMA-only gVAR setup.
Avoid multiple separate CBT-skill variables (e.g., separate reappraisal, metacognitive, and problem-solving) to minimize redundancy—combined into P01.
diagnostics.gvar_feasibility_summary: With 6 criteria + 7 predictors (13 time series) and ~168 momentary observations, an initial gVAR is feasible if adherence is ≥70%. If adherence lower, reduce predictors to top 3–5 (P01, P02, P05, P03 prioritized by likely short-term effects) or use lagged shrinkage priors.

safety_notes: No explicit self-harm or psychosis risk indicated in the complaint. However, intense panic and impairment warrant a recommendation to seek professional evaluation if symptoms include severe functional impairment, syncope, chest pain, or suicidal ideation. Exposure (P03) should be done with safety instructions and stop rules; do not recommend high-intensity interoceptive exposure without clinician oversight. Passive data collection requires informed consent and clear privacy safeguards.

model_summary: Targeting chronic shoulder/jaw tension and tension-linked headaches driven by persistent anticipatory threat and habitual bracing. Choose a compact BIO-PSYCHO-SOCIAL predictor set that (1) reduces peripheral muscle tension (PMR, ergonomics, micro-breaks, wearable cues), (2) increases early detection/interoception (body-scan), and (3) reduces threat expectation/habitual bracing through cognitive reappraisal and social support; sleep protection included as a systemic moderator. Measurements combine 5x/day momentary EMA (1–9 Likert) + 1x/day morning diary for sleep + passive wearable bruxism/jaw-clench signal aggregated per prompt and daily. This design preserves the complaint's resolution (C01–C06), is estimable for gVAR with ~140 momentary observations across 28 days, and minimizes redundant predictors while covering main mechanisms.
variable_selection_notes: Selected criteria (C01–C06) follow the complaint decomposition to preserve distinct constructs: muscle-level tension (C01), interoceptive awareness (C02), headache events (C03), anticipatory threat (C04), relaxation ability in low-stress contexts (C05), and habitual bracing (C06). Predictors were chosen from high-ranking HyDe/LLM signals emphasizing mindfulness/body-scan, PMR, breathing/micro-breaks, cognitive reappraisal, ergonomics, sleep, wearables for bruxism, and social support. Trade-offs: kept both PMR and micro-breaks (different dose/frequency profiles) but avoided adding many overlapping downregulation techniques (e.g., multiple breathing variants) to limit collinearity. Uncertainty/bias: passive jaw-clench sensor availability varies by user; self-report measures rely on subjective scaling. Prioritize within-person 1–9 Likert items to maximize variance for gVAR.

momentary_ema.notes: Use small jitter within each block to avoid predictability. Prompts ask core criteria + selected predictor adherence/intensity items. Average completion target >= 70% prompts.

daily_diary.notes: Morning diary captures prior-night sleep quality, sleep duration, and global baseline for the day (sleep protection adherence).

passive_streams.notes: If wearable with bruxism/jaw-clench detection is available, aggregate clench-episode counts and average intensity in the 'since last prompt' window; also provide daily totals. If no wearable, fall back to EMA self-report bracing flags.

design_recommendations.gvar_readiness_notes: Target ~140 momentary samples; after quality control aim for >=100 usable timepoints. Expect nonstationarity (linear trends or learning effects); detrend (person-specific linear detrending) then within-person z-scoring before gVAR. For count/zero-inflated headache signals use log1p or two-part models (event flag + intensity).
design_recommendations.variance_support_notes: Use 1–9 Likert for momentary constructs to maximize within-person variance. For headache use two-field approach (binary event since-last-prompt + intensity 1–9 if event). Passive clench counts expected to provide additional variance for bracing detection.
design_recommendations.missingness_risk_notes: Main risks: prompt fatigue, wearable dropout, sensor pairing issues. Mitigation: brief items, adherence nudges, allow short fallback self-report for passive signals, and imputation strategies (last-observation-carried-forward not recommended; use model-based interpolation).

criteria_variables.label: Elevated resting muscle tension in shoulders and jaw

measurement.item_or_signal: Since the last prompt, how tense were your shoulders and jaw? (1 = not at all tense, 9 = extremely tense)
measurement.notes: Primary momentary outcome. Also complemented by passive bruxism/clench sensor when available.

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: within-person linear detrend then z-score; treat as approx_continuous (1-9)

criteria_variables.label: Low moment-to-moment awareness of bodily tension

measurement.item_or_signal: Since the last prompt, how aware were you of bodily tension or tightness (shoulders, jaw or neck)? (1 = not at all aware, 9 = very aware)
measurement.notes: Interoceptive awareness; higher scores are salutogenic. Important mediator for early detection.

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: within-person z-score; consider modelling as continuous

measurement.item_or_signal: Since the last prompt, did you have a headache? (Yes/No). If yes, rate intensity: 1 = very mild, 9 = very severe.
measurement.notes: Expect zero-inflation (many no-headache prompts). Use two-part modelling or log1p intensity for gVAR.

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: model binary event separately; log1p(intensity) or ordinal modelling for intensity

measurement.item_or_signal: Right now / Since the last prompt, how much did you expect something bad to happen? (1 = not at all, 9 = extremely)
measurement.notes: Key psychological driver of habitual bracing; target for cognitive reappraisal interventions.

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: within-person z-score; consider autoregressive components in gVAR

measurement.item_or_signal: Since the last prompt, how hard was it for you to relax (physically or mentally)? (1 = not at all hard, 9 = extremely hard)
measurement.notes: Also capture perceived situational stress in the same prompt (single extra item) to identify 'low-stress' contexts for subgroup analyses.

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: within-person z-score; interact with perceived situational stress variable to identify low-stress contexts

criteria_variables.label: Habitual anticipatory muscle bracing in absence of immediate threat

measurement.item_or_signal: Since the last prompt, how much did you notice automatic bracing/tensing (muscles activating without a clear reason)? (1 = none, 9 = constant unintended bracing)
measurement.notes: Complement with passive jaw-clench sensor counts when available to reduce self-report bias.

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: within-person z-score; consider modelling alongside passive clench counts to separate perception vs behavior

predictor_variables.label: Progressive muscle relaxation (PMR) practice (shoulders/jaw-focused)
predictor_variables.mechanism_rationale: Scheduled PMR trains peripheral muscle downregulation and provides a competing relaxation response to habitual bracing, reducing baseline tension and downstream headache triggers.
predictor_variables.intervention_examples_safe: Daily 10–20 minute guided shoulders/jaw PMR sessions; short 3–5 minute targeted PMR micro-sessions.

measurement.item_or_signal: Since the last prompt, did you do a PMR/targeted muscle relaxation practice? If yes, estimate total minutes and rate how fully you engaged (1-9).
measurement.notes: Capture both occurrence (adherence) and engagement/intensity. Use summed minutes per day for dose-response.

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: minutes: log1p for skew; engagement: z-score

expected_effects.rationale: PMR reduces peripheral muscle tension within minutes to hours.
Reduced muscle tension decreases tension-type headache probability over hours.

feasibility.notes: High feasibility via brief EMA; self-report minutes may be noisy.

predictor_variables.label: Interoceptive body-scan / mindful-body awareness check-ins
predictor_variables.mechanism_rationale: Improves moment-to-moment detection of tension (increasing C02), enabling earlier self-regulation and reducing downstream bracing/headache episodes.
predictor_variables.intervention_examples_safe: Brief guided 1–3 minute body-checks deployed as a notification or self-initiated body-scan practice.

measurement.item_or_signal: Since the last prompt, did you do a body awareness or body-scan check-in? If yes, how long (minutes) and how attentive did you feel (1-9)?
measurement.notes: Key proximal predictor of increased interoceptive awareness (C02).

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: minutes: log1p; attention: z-score

expected_effects.rationale: Body-scan increases awareness of bodily signals within minutes to hours.

feasibility.notes: High within-person variance expected; adherence may decline without reminders.

predictor_variables.label: Hourly micro-breaks with gentle movement + diaphragmatic breathing
predictor_variables.mechanism_rationale: Frequent short breaks reduce accumulated muscle tension and autonomic arousal; combined movement prevents postural stiffness while breathing downshifts arousal.
predictor_variables.intervention_examples_safe: 1–3 minute standing stretch + 6-breath diaphragmatic breathing every hour; phone reminders to cue micro-breaks.

measurement.item_or_signal: Since the last prompt, did you take a micro-break (movement + breathing)? If yes, how many times and rate effectiveness (1-9).
measurement.notes: Short frequent actions expected to show immediate (0-6h) effects on tension and relaxation.

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: counts: square-root or log1p; effectiveness z-score

expected_effects.rationale: Micro-breaks quickly increase ability to relax and reduce stiffness.

feasibility.notes: Feasible; may rely on user action and reminders for adherence.

predictor_variables.label: Threat reappraisal / cognitive reframing practice
predictor_variables.mechanism_rationale: Active cognitive reappraisal reduces persistent anticipatory threat expectations, thereby reducing mental tension and secondary bracing.
predictor_variables.intervention_examples_safe: Brief 1–2 question reappraisal prompts (e.g., probability and coping re-evaluation) and short cognitive exercises to consider alternative, less-threatening interpretations.

measurement.item_or_signal: Since the last prompt, did you try a reappraisal/cognitive reframing exercise? If yes, rate how helpful it felt (1-9).
measurement.notes: Expected to change anticipatory threat over hours to days; effect size on muscle tension likely indirect.

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: binary flag as indicator; helpfulness z-score

expected_effects.rationale: Reappraisal directly targets threat expectation; effects may consolidate across prompts.

feasibility.notes: Adherence may be lower than simple breathing prompts; include micro-exercises to improve uptake.

predictor_variables.label: Ergonomic / posture cueing and adjustments
predictor_variables.mechanism_rationale: Postural improvements and cueing reduce mechanical loading on neck/shoulders and reduce tonic muscle tension and bracing.
predictor_variables.intervention_examples_safe: Adjust chair/monitor height, use posture reminder app, brief posture reset exercises hourly.

measurement.item_or_signal: Since the last prompt, did you adjust posture or follow an ergonomic cue (yes/no)? If yes, rate how well you implemented it (1-9).
measurement.notes: Optional passive posture proxy via phone orientation/accelerometer if available — aggregate per-prompt.

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: flag + quality z-score

expected_effects.rationale: Mechanical/postural changes reduce sustained muscle tension over hours.

feasibility.notes: Feasible but may rely on environmental changes; passive posture data optional.

predictor_variables.label: Sleep protection / circadian routine adherence
predictor_variables.mechanism_rationale: Improved sleep quality reduces baseline arousal, lowers headache susceptibility, and improves daytime relaxation and resilience.
predictor_variables.intervention_examples_safe: Consistent bed/wake times, pre-sleep downshift (no screens 30–60 min), short presleep PMR.

measurement.item_or_signal: Last night: total sleep time (hrs), sleep quality (1-9), time to fall asleep in minutes. Also capture adherence to planned sleep routine (yes/no).
measurement.notes: Moderator variable; daily morning entry reduces recall bias for sleep.

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: sleep time: z-score or raw hours; quality: z-score

expected_effects.rationale: Better sleep reduces headache risk the following day; effects may appear overnight and in daytime.

feasibility.notes: High feasibility via single daily diary item.

predictor_variables.label: Wearable jaw-clench/bruxism sensor (passive) — episodes & intensity
predictor_variables.mechanism_rationale: Objective detection of clench/bracing episodes provides early warning (improves C02 via feedback) and a direct behavioral measure of habitual bracing (C06 & C01). Also enables cue-based interventions.
predictor_variables.intervention_examples_safe: If wearable detects clench episodes, trigger brief cue to release jaw and breathe; use daily summary to inform habit-change.

measurement.channel: wearable device with jaw-clench or EMG proxy (or night bruxism sensor); phone-connected wearable
measurement.item_or_signal: Number of clench/bracing episodes and average intensity in the 'since last prompt' window; daily total episodes and mean intensity.
measurement.response_scale_or_unit: count of episodes; mean intensity (device-specific units)

diagnostics.gvar_feasibility_summary: With 5 prompts/day over 28 days (~140 momentary observations) and a compact set of 6 criteria + 8 predictors, the design is estimable for person-level gVAR after detrending and z-scoring. Expect to drop variables with extensive missingness (e.g., P07 if wearable unavailable). Use two-part modelling for headache signal.

safety_notes: No acute safety concerns signaled in the complaint. If during data collection the participant reports escalating severe or disabling headaches, severe functional impairment, or suicidal ideation, advise immediate clinical contact. Ensure explicit consent and privacy protections for any passive physiological monitoring (P07).

model_summary: Focused, gVAR-ready observation model for an evening-onset bleak/empty mood pattern sustained by prolonged passive smartphone use and perceived effortful initiation. Criteria capture evening-specific subjective mood, low outward expression, evening phone scrolling, perceived energy barriers, and avoidant function of phone use. Predictors are a compact set of modifiable levers: evening behavioral activation, objective screen-time, sleep/circadian routine, social engagement, mindfulness/acceptance coping, perceived initiation self-efficacy, and measurement/feedback engagement. Sampling emphasizes repeated momentary EMA across the day with denser evening coverage plus passive phone metrics and a single morning diary for sleep — designed for within-person variance and later gVAR modeling with recommended detrending/person-mean centering.
variable_selection_notes: Selected the five complaint-derived criteria (C01–C05) to preserve complaint resolution and temporal specificity (evenings). Chose 7 predictors to cover highest-signal interventions from HyDe and LLM mapping: behavioral activation (PSYCHO), screen-time reduction (SOCIAL/DIGITAL passive), sleep/circadian routine (BIO), social engagement (SOCIAL), mindfulness/acceptance coping (PSYCHO), perceived energy/self-efficacy (PSYCHO), and measurement/feedback adherence (BIO/Personalization). Trade-offs: kept a single broad self-regulation predictor (perceived initiation self-efficacy) to avoid near-duplicate constructs (e.g., multiple self-regulation measures). Included one strong passive signal (screen time) despite partial overlap with C03 because it is an actionable, objective predictor useful for stimulus-control interventions. Uncertainty/bias notes: passive screen-time may capture required-but-not-avoidant uses (work, messaging) — treat with context EMA. Sleep variables are daily (lower sampling) and create mixed-frequency data — align by aggregating/lagging for gVAR (see gvar_readiness_notes).

momentary_ema.notes: Denser coverage in evening blocks (16:00 onward) to sample the target window. Jitter ±10–20 minutes to reduce predictability. 'Since last prompt' minimizes recall bias and matches passive per-prompt aggregation.

daily_diary.notes: Morning diary to capture previous night's sleep timing/quality and global night-to-night circadian markers (for P03).

passive_streams.notes: Collect continuous screen-time/unlock counts via platform API and aggregate into 'past 3 hours' windows to align with EMA prompts. Platform API should return minutes active and unlock_count per aggregation window.

design_recommendations.gvar_readiness_notes: Expect diurnal (evening) seasonality and trends; plan person-mean centering and optionally remove weekly trends. For mixed-frequency predictors (daily sleep) align by carrying forward previous morning value or model as a slower node with 24h lag. Use within-person z-scoring for gVAR coefficients comparability. Consider log1p for skewed counts (screen minutes) and Winsorize extreme values. Recommended minimum_total_observations_target adjusted to 140 (<= expected 168) to match achievable within-person sampling; larger samples recommended if estimating very small effects.
design_recommendations.variance_support_notes: Use 1–9 Likert anchors for EMA items to maximize within-person variance and approximate continuity. Passive screen minutes retained as near-continuous but may be skewed; transform as needed.
design_recommendations.missingness_risk_notes: Highest missingness expected in late-evening prompts; mitigate via passive backups (screen-time) and reminders/incentives. Expect occasional passive permission loss — check consent and permissions at enrollment.

diagnostics.gvar_feasibility_summary: With 28 days × 5 momentary prompts = ~140 momentary observations plus 28 daily sleep entries and passive per-prompt aggregates, expect adequate data for within-person gVAR if missingness <30% and minimum_total_observations_target of ~140 is met. Mixed-frequency alignment (daily sleep vs momentary EMA) is required; treat sleep as a slower-moving node with 12–24h lags. Larger samples recommended for detecting very small lagged effects.

safety_notes: No explicit acute safety signals in the complaint text. If at any point the participant reports severe hopelessness, suicidal ideation, or significant functional decline, advise immediate professional help and provide emergency resources. Ensure explicit informed consent for passive data collection and privacy protections.

model_summary: Initial observation model for postpartum emotional distress focusing on eight operationalized criteria (identity change, bonding, entrapment, guilt, overwhelm, tearfulness, self-criticism, perceived invalidation). Predictors prioritized to be modifiable and measurable at the EMA / daily / passive timescales: sleep (subjective + passive), self-compassion practice, cognitive reappraisal use, perceived social validation, respite hours, physical activity (steps), and social-media/screen exposure. Design emphasizes within-person variance (1–9 Likert where possible), coherent sampling (6 EMA prompts/day for momentary states, 1 daily morning diary for sleep/trait items, passive daily/ per-prompt aggregation), and parsimony to support later gVAR estimation while preserving coverage of BIO/PSYCHO/SOCIAL levers.
variable_selection_notes: Selected criteria preserve full complaint resolution (all 8 original criterion variables retained). Predictor selection prioritized high HyDe/LLM signals and mechanistic plausibility: sleep (top-ranked in LLM mappings), self-compassion/emotion-regulation (HyDe top-ranked), cognitive reappraisal/behavioral activation (CBT signals), social support/respite (SOCIAL signals), activity and passive digital exposures as contextual modifiers. Trade-offs: limited number of overlapping constructs to reduce collinearity (chose one broad self-compassion measure and one cognitive-reappraisal measure rather than multiple overlapping self-regulation items). Passive signals chosen to increase objective variance but are optional if device access is lacking. Uncertainty/bias notes: identity-change (C01) is more trait-like (low within-person variability) and will likely require detrending or differencing; perceived bonding (C02) shows ceiling risk; screen-time effects are potentially nonlinear, so we model them as such rather than forcing monotonic signs.

momentary_ema.notes: Fixed 6 blocks with small random jitter to reduce predictability and memory biases. Use single-screen short EMA (<30s) per prompt to minimize burden.

daily_diary.notes: Morning diary (07:30) capturing last-night sleep subjective rating and previous day's respite hours / summary of major events. Use brief structured items.

passive_streams.aggregation_strategy: passive_continuous then aggregate per-prompt and daily
passive_streams.notes: Collect continuous phone/wearable-derived steps and screen-time and sleep-tracking where available. Aggregate steps and screen-time into 'since last prompt' windows for per-prompt alignment; sleep minutes aggregated nightly for morning diary.

design_recommendations.gvar_readiness_notes: Expect ~168 momentary + 28 daily observations; after preprocessing (imputation, detrending, z-scoring), gVAR with this individual time series is feasible. Control for nonstationarity (C01 low within-person variance) by detrending or treating C01 as slow-moving exogenous or using difference series. Consider orthogonalizing or selecting between highly collinear sleep signals (P01 vs P02) to improve gVAR stability. Use person-mean centering for predictors and criteria before VAR modeling.
design_recommendations.variance_support_notes: Use 1-9 Likert scales for subjective constructs to increase variance. Passive step/screen aggregates will add continuous variability. Encourage wearing phone/wearable and enable OS sleep tracking to support objective sleep minutes.
design_recommendations.missingness_risk_notes: EMA burden (6/day) may induce missingness; implement brief prompts, user reminders, and allow Grace windows. Passive streams mitigate missingness but depend on permissions and device availability; collect meta-data on missingness and device status.

measurement.item_or_signal: Since the last prompt, how supported or understood did you feel by others? (1 = not at all, 9 = completely supported)
measurement.notes: Captures social-context fluctuations; targets perceived minimization (C08) directly.

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: person-mean centering

Perceived support alleviates feelings of entrapment by enabling resource sharing.

predictor_variables.risk_ethics_notes: Asking about social support may surface relationship issues; provide referral options if distress increases.

feasibility.notes: Simple EMA item; likely high compliance.

predictor_variables.intervention_examples_safe: Scheduling short breaks, arranging family/friend or paid childcare, micro-respite planning.

measurement.notes: Captured in morning diary as sum for previous day; can be sensitive; anonymize if storing free-text.

expected_effects.rationale: Respite has immediate effect on perceived entrapment and overwhelm.
Respite increases coping capacity quickly.

predictor_variables.risk_ethics_notes: Asking about childcare access may reveal lack of resources; provide resources/referral options.

feasibility.notes: Daily reporting reduces recall bias vs. momentary.

predictor_variables.label: Physical activity (steps per prompt-window)

predictor_variables.intervention_examples_safe: Short walks, paced activity during baby naps.

measurement.notes: Aggregate into per-prompt windows to align with EMA.

predictor_variables.risk_ethics_notes: Passive step data requires permission; may be missing if phone not carried.

feasibility.notes: Useful objective covariate but optional if devices unavailable.

predictor_variables.intervention_examples_safe: Time-limited social-media use, curated content, brief social-media breaks.
